questions.question: What is the number of sides in a hexagon?
What is the name of the polygon with 10 sides?
How many vertices does a sphere have?
questions.explanation: A sphere is a three-dimensional shape with one vertex.

questions.question: What is the formula for the sum of interior angles in a polygon?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a polygon can be calculated using the formula (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides.

questions.question: What is the name of the solid with a circular base?
questions.explanation: A cylinder has a circular base and straight lateral surface.

questions.question: How many sides does an octagon have?
What is the name of the polygon with five sides?
How many faces does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube has six faces, each of which is a square.

questions.question: What is the name of the three-dimensional shape with four right angles and four sides of equal length?
questions.explanation: A square pyramid has four right angles and four sides of equal length.

questions.question: What is the name of the three-dimensional shape with curved lateral surface?
questions.explanation: A cone has a circular base and curved lateral surface.

notes.title: Properties of Regular Polygons
notes.content: Regular polygons have equal sides and equal angles. The sum of interior angles in a polygon can be calculated using the formula (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides.

notes.title: Types of Three-Dimensional Shapes
notes.content: Three-dimensional shapes include cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres. Each type has its own unique properties and characteristics.

notes.title: Sum of Interior Angles in a Polygon
notes.content: The sum of interior angles in a polygon can be calculated using the formula (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides.

notes.title: Properties of Circumference and Diameter
notes.content: The circumference of a circle is given by the formula C = 2πr, and the diameter is twice the radius. The area of a circle can be calculated using the formula A = πr^2.

notes.title: Types of Polygons
notes.content: Polygons are classified into regular polygons and irregular polygons. Regular polygons have equal sides and equal angles, while irregular polygons do not.

notes.title: Properties of Prisms and Pyramids
notes.content: Prisms and pyramids are three-dimensional shapes with two bases and lateral surfaces. The sum of interior angles in a prism or pyramid can be calculated using the formula (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides.

notes.title: Properties of Cylinders
notes.content: Cylinders have a circular base and straight lateral surface. The sum of interior angles in a cylinder can be calculated using the formula (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides.

notes.title: Properties of Spheres
notes.content: Spheres are three-dimensional shapes with one vertex. The surface area and volume of a sphere can be calculated using specific formulas.

notes.title: Properties of Cones
notes.content: Cones have a circular base and curved lateral surface. The sum of interior angles in a cone can be calculated using the formula (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides.

notes.title: Properties of Right-Angled Triangles
notes.content: Right-angled triangles have one right angle. The Pythagorean theorem can be used to calculate the length of the hypotenuse.

facts.fact: A hexagon has six sides and six angles.
The sum of interior angles in a polygon can be calculated using the formula (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides.
A sphere has one vertex and a curved surface.
The circumference of a circle is given by the formula C = 2πr, where r is the radius.
A cylinder has two parallel and circular bases with straight lateral surface.
